column6: baked-in character silhouette at bottom-center (head/face/shoulders visible as skin-tone pixels) — tiles must be tileable; a person inside breaks every wall instance in the game. Also: no trim/rivets, stylistically inconsistent with the rest of the wall family. Reroll without any figure, match panel+trim vocabulary of corner/cross/straight.

REGRESSION. Old had distinctive reactor-core iconography (blue circuit/fan rosette, orange accents); current is flat dark grey, indistinguishable from tile.floor. No reactor motif remains. Reroll with legible core visual — hex grid + radial vents + cool-blue glow accents, or simplified reactor-chevron.

REGRESSION. Old had security-checkpoint decal (blue cross pattern + red-orange central lock); current is flat dark grey, indistinguishable from tile.floor. Reroll with legible security motif — lock, chevron alert bands, or red/blue checkpoint ring.

REROLL to old-style subtle starfield. Per bmo render grep + tinyclaw: chromatic colored pixels (orange/cyan/blue/red squares) read as nebula-debris, not stars, and will show visible 256x256 seam repetition when tiled as background. Target: pure black base, sparse 1px white/pale-blue stars at low density, verified tileable on all 4 edges (left-right + top-bottom continuous).

SPRITE IS MISSING/BLANK. The current atlas crop at frame bounds (930,262,128,128) shows only near-black navy with faint grid artifact lines — no cabinet, no screen, no controls, no iconography whatsoever. Either the gen failed silently, the crop frame is wrong, or the sprite was written fully transparent. Hard reject — cannot approve an empty sprite. Reroll to match module.rec_unit style (front-facing arcade/game cabinet with distinctive gaming iconography: multi-colored screen, controllers/paddles, visually distinct from rec_unit).

REROLL: medical motif too subtle — small monitor icon + mattress-color-only differentiation doesn't survive 64px game-scale rendering (monitor goes to 1-2px blob per tinyclaw). Reroll spec: top-down bed silhouette consistent with module.bed, but with high-contrast medical iconography taking ≥15% of sprite area. Options: white+red-cross pattern on pillow/mattress, red-accent frame rim, or head-side clipboard/IV occupying 15-20% sprite. Rimworld convention = red+white palette dominates medical furniture.

SEAT-COUNT TOO SPARSE. Sprite is 128×128 = 2×2 tile footprint but only has 2 chairs (on long sides). 2×2 dining tables should seat 4 (chairs on N/E/S/W per rimworld convention). Current wastes visual space + reads as "narrow 2-seat table placed oddly in a 2×2 area." Reroll spec: top-down 4-seat dining table, square/round table centered, one chair on each side (N/E/S/W), matches old atlas seat-count but keep the simplified top-down style of the current chairs.

GEN FAILURE — gen pipeline did not produce direction-specific or variant-specific dock facades. Pixel-hash analysis showed east.*, south.*, west.* each have all 4 variants (end/middle/solo/start) PIXEL-IDENTICAL within the direction = same placeholder sprite 4x. north.start + north.end are visually near-identical to the east/south/west placeholder too (diff only 123k-205k pixels on a 64x64 = minor per-pixel tweaks, not a real variant). Only north.solo + north.middle are actual dock-facade sprites. REROLL PLAN: either (a) gen proper 4-direction × 4-variant = 16 unique sprites using north.solo + north.middle as style anchors, with orientation rotated per direction and variant purposes distinct (solo=standalone bay, start=left-edge, middle=repeating mid, end=right-edge); or (b) simpler — delete the 14 placeholder keys from atlas + manifest, route render code to rotate north.* sprites for other directions (cleaner + catches the "we don't actually need 16 sprites" insight).

GEN DUPLICATION — pixel-diff analysis: wall.exterior.3 = corner.1 = end.1 (zero diff, pixel-identical); wall.exterior.2 is near-identical to that cluster (21k diff = anti-aliasing noise). Only wall.exterior.1 is a genuinely-distinct sprite. corner.1 and end.1 especially SHOULD be visually distinct variants (an outside-corner L-bracket shape, an end-cap shape) but came out as duplicates of the straight bracket. REROLL PLAN: either (a) gen proper 3 tiling variants (.1/.2/.3 with real pixel variety) + a corner-L variant + an end-cap variant, OR (b) follow same simplification as dock facade: delete 4 duplicate keys, keep only wall.exterior.1 as the single exterior bracket, let code handle corner/end via rotation or post-processing.
